body.title: Fable consult — coding-loop sequencing plan (2026-07-04)
body.text:
## What / why
After the aider/SWE-agent survey + shipping the 3 redesign levers (architect/editor split, signature map, minion ACI) + a confirm-by that left DS.0 at 0 edits, Akien asked for a top-of-stack PLAN (not a build) from a fresh strong model. Consulted claude-fable-5 as a Plan architect with the loop code + confirm-by evidence + the copilot/build-packet ticket (T-uu-build-packet-compiler) + the north star (DS.0 leveling). Its plan reordered our A/B/C fork.

## The killer insight (reframes our confirm-by attribution)
We have been optimizing the INPUT side of a pipeline whose OUTPUT side has never once been observed to work. devstral made 0 Write/Edit attempts in BOTH runs. Run 1 we blamed orientation-drowning; run 2 the Hex timeout. That attribution is UNTESTED — equally consistent with devstral being unable to serialize exact-string Edit calls under this ACI at all. Delta's 51%/89% are input-token reductions, not completions. So: prove the output half works before investing more in the input half.

## Verdict: both A and B, interleaved — the build packet is the architect's INPUT, not a replacement for the split. But two cheap signal steps come first.

## Sequence (what we adopted)
1. Hex timeout fix (STEP 1, gates all signal) — derive an effective_timeout from source cost class, mirroring the existing flat_rate turn-cap pattern (agentic_loop.py ~L660-665); raise at the turn-0 flat_rate lock (base 120 arbitrary; local $0 box waiting is free -> 480-600s). Secondary cause: ARCHITECT_PROMPT demands full exact old/new strings -> longest generation in the system on slowest hardware; softening plan format cuts it. -> T-ds-hex-dispatch-timeout-midloop.
2. Editor-isolation smoke test (cheapest whole-design falsifier) — hand-write a 2-3 edit plan for a closed ticket, feed it straight into the editor half on Hex, bypass the architect. PASS -> build-packet trunk validated; FAIL -> the bug is the edit interface/ACI or per-role tiering, pivot there. -> T-ds-editor-isolation-smoke-test (NEW, gated on #1).
3. Build-packet compiler v1 (the copilot tickets) — extend pre_inference_assemble.py into build_packet.py (sufficiency_gate, token_measurement, fingerprint, schema). The packet ABSORBS the signature map: CodingDomain._initial_message replaces its build_signature_map() prefix with the packet's context_shortlist (fed by query_file_symbols). One orientation artifact, not two. -> T-uu-build-packet-compiler (wiring note added; gated behind #2).
4. Shrink the architect to a near-single-call planner (turn budget <=8; exploration is a smell) once orientation lives in the packet. Wire per-role tiering (qwen3-coder:30b architect / devstral editor) if #2 shows planning quality is the limiter.
5. Funnel batch: 5-10 real S tickets end-to-end; record per ticket gate-pass -> plan -> edits attempted -> edits applied -> tests pass -> DONE. This funnel becomes the standing metric.
6. Only if the funnel says orientation is still the leak: dependency edges in code_index (signature-map ranking is keyword-ILIKE only) + gate-blocking promotion.

## Sharpest risk Fable named
Zero edits attributed to orientation/latency is untested; if step 2 fails, the whole input-side roadmap is aimed at the wrong stage — and it costs an afternoon to find out.

## Signals to watch
- After #1: architect reaches LOOP_DONE (plan envelope) instead of availability-midrun-wall-halt; editor loop starts. Watch architect wall-clock + output tokens.
- After #2: first nonzero Edit/Write attempts + edit-success ratio (OK:edited vs old_string not found / matches N). This one number says whether the editor half is viable.
- After #3: architect turn count drops (<=5); identical input -> identical fingerprint_sha256; gate fails loudly on an underspecified fixture; measured orientation-token reduction >30%.
- Whole-bet confirm: one ticket goes gate-pass -> plan -> applied edits -> green tests -> DONE with zero human touches on Hex. Falsifier: edits attempted but chronically failing exact-string match (interface problem) or plans produced but never followed (planner-editor capability gap).
